text.title: Schedule Development Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules — every activity-list artefact must satisfy all five.

rule.statement: Every activity MUST be verb-led and describe work, not deliverable. 'Implement ingest pipeline' is accepted; 'Ingest pipeline' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Verb-led activities make actor + action explicit; noun-only entries blur work and product scope.

rule.statement: Every activity with duration over 5 days MUST carry three-point (O/M/P) estimates; single-point estimates over 5d are rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-point estimates create false precision; PERT captures pessimistic scenarios where late projects originate.

rule.statement: Every predecessor link MUST declare type: FS, SS, FF, or SF. Untyped links are rejected.
rule.rationale: Default FS assumed silently breaks resource-levelling and lag computation.

rule.statement: Buffers MUST be held at project and feeding-chain level, named and visible. Per-activity hidden padding is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hidden padding creates buffers no one can manage; visible buffers enable Goldratt-style monitoring.

rule.statement: The schedule MUST be refreshed at every weekly status cycle; schedules with `last_refreshed` > 14 days are flagged stale.
rule.rationale: Critical path drifts; stale schedules represent kickoff state, not today.
